config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent Indonesia vs China native-or-English block. Each persona speaks only English or its own native language.
config.cell_description: One-side-native cross cell: Indonesia persona speaks Indonesian; China persona speaks English.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: One-side-native cross cell: Indonesia persona speaks Indonesian; China persona speaks English.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: TIDAK SETUJU. Menurut saya, hak individu tidak boleh dikalahkan begitu saja atas nama kepentingan masyarakat, karena justru masyarakat yang sehat dibangun dari perlindungan terhadap martabat, kebebasan, dan keadilan bagi tiap orang. Saya bisa menerima pembatasan hak individu hanya dalam keadaan tertentu, misalnya untuk mencegah bahaya nyata bagi orang banyak, tetapi itu harus ketat, proporsional, dan tidak sewenang-wenang. Kalau kepentingan masyarakat selalu ditempatkan di atas hak individu, mudah sekali terjadi penyalahgunaan oleh negara atau kelompok yang berkuasa.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not support the idea that society should automatically come before the individual, because that approach can easily become an excuse for abuse of power. From my perspective as someone from China, I understand why people emphasize social stability and collective interests, but I also think individual rights need real protection or they become too easy to sacrifice in the name of the greater good. A society is not truly healthy if it treats people as tools for abstract public goals, especially when the supposed “societal interest” is defined by those in authority. I would only accept limiting individual rights when it is necessary, proportional, and clearly accountable.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus selalu diprioritaskan di atas hak individu. Dari pengalaman melihat konteks Indonesia, alasan “demi kepentingan umum” terlalu sering dipakai untuk membenarkan pembatasan yang berlebihan, padahal hak individu justru perlu dilindungi supaya negara tidak semena-mena. Kepentingan bersama memang penting, tetapi harus dicapai dengan batas yang jelas, proporsional, dan bisa dipertanggungjawabkan, bukan dengan mengorbankan martabat orang per orang. Kalau hak individu dianggap sekunder terus-menerus, yang kuat akan lebih mudah menentukan apa yang disebut “kepentingan masyarakat.”

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not believe the interests of society should automatically take priority over the rights of the individual. From my perspective as someone from China, I understand why people value collective order and social stability, but I have also seen how easily “the public interest” can be used to justify overreach when individual rights are treated as secondary. A society is stronger when it protects people from arbitrary power, not when it expects them to sacrifice their rights whenever authority says it is necessary. I would only accept limits on individual rights when they are truly necessary, narrowly targeted, and clearly accountable.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap tidak setuju bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus didahulukan secara umum di atas hak individu. Dalam konteks Indonesia, alasan “demi kepentingan umum” terlalu sering dipakai untuk membenarkan pembatasan yang longgar, padahal justru di situlah bahaya penyalahgunaan kekuasaan muncul. Kepentingan bersama memang penting, tetapi tidak boleh dijadikan alasan untuk menginjak martabat, kebebasan, dan perlindungan hukum tiap orang. Saya hanya bisa menerima pembatasan hak individu kalau benar-benar perlu, proporsional, dan ada pengawasan yang jelas.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not think society should take priority over the individual as a general rule. From my perspective as someone from China, I understand the appeal of collective stability, but I also know how easily “societal interest” can be used to silence ordinary people when there is too little accountability. In practice, a society that treats individual rights as optional often ends up protecting power, not the public. I would only put society first in narrow cases where there is a clear and serious harm, and even then the limitation must be strictly justified and controlled.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
